macro_segments.title: The Boat Case: Landing Within or Outside the Two-Thousand Cubit Boundary

micro_segments.title: Setting up the boat case and Rabban Gamliel's solution
The gemara's enquiry: can they debark if within two thousand cubits?
Rabban Gamliel's optical device (shfoferet) for measuring two thousand cubits on land and sea
Rashi's explanation of the tube: it focuses vision to a fixed distance
Alternative interpretations: surveying instruments and mathematical angles
Using the device to measure valley depth and palm tree height via shadow proportions
Preventing evil animals from resting in grave shadows by measuring and building mounds

macro_segments.title: Nechemya's Distraction and the Solution of Surrounding with People

micro_segments.title: Nechemya absorbed in study, walks outside the techum involuntarily
Rav Nachman's solution: surround him with people to create an enclosed space
This ruling depends on following Rabban Gamliel's position on enclosed spaces
Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak questions whether the solution works or if there is a halachic issue
The underlying problem: whether they can reach him or only get within two cubits via Rabbi Eliezer's position

macro_segments.title: Can a Wall Be Made from People on Shabbat? Reconciling Contradictory Braitot

micro_segments.title: First braita: cannot rebuild a sukkah wall with people, animals, or vessels on Yom Tov
Second braita: you may use your friend as a wall for a sukkah to eat, drink, and sleep
Reconciling the contradiction: the first braita forbids making the third wall; the second allows non-halachic walls
Debate between Rabbi Meir and Rabbis: does an animal wall count as halachic or not?
The true resolution: third wall is forbidden; fourth wall or separate shade-providing wall is permitted

macro_segments.title: Halachic vs. Non-Halachic Walls: The Significance of Awareness and Intent

micro_segments.title: Definition of halachic wall: one that serves a halachic function in defining a reshut or mechitza
Non-halachic walls permitted on Shabbat: those providing shade or temporary shelter without formal definition
Example: mechitza in shul for women—permitted if viewed as practical separation, not formal division of reshuyot
The people-as-wall solution: permissible only if people are unaware they are serving as a mechitza
Examples of using unaware people to bring items within techum on Shabbat (water, straw, fodder)
Conclusion: awareness of halachic purpose makes the wall creation prohibited as building on Shabbat

macro_segments.title: Practical Application: Techum Above Ten Tefachim and Landing on Shabbat

micro_segments.title: No techum applies above ten tefachim because it is not d'oraita in mid-air or on water
If a plane lands on Shabbat and remains above ten tefachim during taxiing, techum begins where it lands
Shulchan Aruch ruling: passenger on boat above ten tefachim has two thousand cubits from landing point
Practical case: cruise ships and vessels landing on Shabbat benefit from two thousand cubit techum at landing

macro_segments.title: One Who Left with Permission for a Mitzvah, Then Is No Longer Needed

micro_segments.title: Mishna setup: one permitted to leave techum for a mitzvah (midwife, testimony) gets two thousand cubits
Problem: if told services no longer needed mid-journey, stuck with only two thousand cubits from that spot
If still within original techum, it is as if one never left; techum reverts to original
Gemara clarifies: overlapping techumim—if new two thousand cubits overlap original, may return home
Debate: does overlap of techumim create one enlarged techum (Rabbah/Rashmi) or remain separate?
Cave example: if begun Shabbat at cave bottom surrounded by walls, techum extends from both exits

macro_segments.title: One Taken Out Against His Will: Gentiles or Insanity; The Fold and Corral Dispute

micro_segments.title: Mishna: if removed by gentiles or bad spirit (insanity), only four cubits permitted
If returned to original place, treated as if never left
Dispute: if placed in fold (dir) or corral (sahar), can walk entire enclosure or limited to four cubits?
Rabban Gamliel and Rabbi Elazar ben Azaryah: full enclosure allows walking entire space
Rabbi Yehoshua and Rabbi Akiva: limited to four cubits even in enclosure
Practical case: ship from Brindisi—first pair walked entire ship; second pair limited selves to four cubits voluntarily

topical_tags.term: Chutz l'techum (outside the two thousand cubit boundary)
Rabban Gamliel (position on enclosed spaces and techum)
Rabbi Meir vs. Rabbis (animal wall debate)
Awareness/da'at (whether people know they serve as wall)
Techum above ten tefachim (in air or on water)
Mitzvah departure (leaving techum for rescue or testimony)
Rabbi Yehoshua vs. Rabban Gamliel (on enclosed spaces)
